message:
perf(query): precomputed IN-list HashSet cache and MemDB direct equality scan for prepared queries

connection.rs (bd-wwqen.2 HashSet cache):
- New PrecomputedInSetCache struct holding HashSet<SqliteValueHashable>
  and has_null flag for three-valued NULL logic
- New precomputed_in_sets field on Connection (RefCell<HashMap>)
  caches HashSets keyed by Vec<Expr> heap pointer address
- On first probe of a large literal IN list (>16 elements), build
  and cache the HashSet. All subsequent outer rows reuse the
  cached set for O(1) membership test instead of rebuilding O(N)
  per row
- Cache cleared at the start of each rewrite_in_expr call

connection.rs (bd-wwqen.4 MemDB direct scan):
- SimpleIndexedEqualityLookup fast-path now has a direct MemDB
  equality scan implementation that bypasses the VDBE engine
  entirely — iterates MemDB table rows, compares via
  values_equal_sqlite, patches IPK alias column with actual rowid,
  returns Row/NoRows/MultipleRows directly

in_exists_regression.rs (5 new tests):
- Three-valued NULL logic above 16-element threshold (found+NULL
  =TRUE, not-found+NULL=NULL, not-found+no-NULL=FALSE)
- Duplicates do not break membership semantics
- Mixed Integer/Float cross-type equality (5 matches 5.0)
- Text case sensitivity (binary comparison, no NOCASE)
- NOT IN with NULLs (mirror of positive IN tests)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
